sentence: The build-up of snow where more falls than melts is called ____.
Dense, old snow that has not yet turned into ice is called ____.
Glacial erosion where rocks frozen into the ice scrape along the valley floor is called ____.
Erosion where ice freezes onto rock and pulls pieces away is called ____.
A sharp ridge formed between two corries is called an ____.
The armchair-shaped hollow where a glacier begins is called a ____.
Unsorted material deposited directly by a glacier is called ____.
Material carried and deposited by a glacier along its sides is called lateral ____.
Material deposited at the furthest point a glacier reached is called ____ moraine.
A smooth, rounded, elongated hill of till deposited beneath a glacier is called a ____.
